name: Crepes Stuffed with Poblano Chiles and Spinach
recipeIngredient: 1 cup polenta or finely ground cornmeal
1/4 teaspoon kosher salt or sea salt
3 fresh green poblano chiles, cored and seeded
3 cups spinach, packed, washed, and dried fresh
1 teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes or to taste
Cholula Hot Sauce or other Mexican or Louisiana-style hot sauce such as Buffalo or Tabasco
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium-sized bowl, combine the polenta, salt, and cayenne pepper.
2. In a separate bowl, beat the eggs with water until well blended.
3. Make a well in the center of the dry ingredients and add the egg mixture slowly, beating constantly with a fork or whisk to avoid lumps. The mixture will be thin.
4. Lightly grease a large skillet for the first batch of crepes only. Warm the skillet over medium-low heat.
5. Pour more than 2 tablespoons of batter into the skillet for each crepe, forming circles about 5 inches in diameter.
6. Cook on one side only until the top of the crepe is set. Transfer cooked side down to cool on a sheet of waxed paper. Repeat until all the batter has been used. You should end up with 16 to 20 crepes, which can cool while you make the filling.
7. Quarter the poblanos and place in a food processor along with chile oil and water. Blend until peppers are finely chopped. Add spinach and process again until well blended.
8. Turn vegetable mixture into a medium-sized bowl and add cheeses, eggs, and seasonings. Mix well, adjusting salt and pepper to taste.
9. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
10. Spread about 2 to 3 tablespoons of the vegetable mixture along the center of each crepe. Roll up the crepe and place seam side down in a lightly greased 9 by 13-inch ceramic baking dish, nesting them closely together in one layer.
11. Top with 1 1/2 cups Monterey jack cheese and sprinkle generously but not soaking with hot sauce.
12. Bake for 25 minutes. Serve with hot sauce on the side.
The crepes are easy to make; the entire dish can be assembled in about 40 minutes.
